name: Puerta de Ancares Loop
name_original: La Puerta de Ancares desde Pionedo — circular por la Sierra de Los Ancares
description: This difficult 39.6-mile mountain biking route from Pionedo offers challenging climbs, technical descents, and stunning views in Sierra de L
region_intro: Mountain biking in Ancares offers challenging terrain across a mountainous region in northwest Spain. The landscape features prominent peaks, deep valleys carved by rivers, and diverse vegetation including extensive forests of oak, beech, and chestnut. This UNESCO Biosphere Reserve provides a mix of flowy singletrack and technical sections with steep gradients and rocky paths. The region's rugged character makes it suitable for mountain bikers seeking demanding routes. Best mountain bike trails in Ancares The most popular mountain bike trail is Puerta de Ancares Loop, a 39.6 miles (63.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied mountain landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail, a difficult 21.9 miles (35.3 km) path. This route climbs to high elevations, offering expansive views of the Ancares mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail leading through the Los Ancares-Courel Special Conservation Area, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking in Ancares is defined by rugged mountainous terrain, deep river valleys, and extensive forests. The network offers options for advanced riders seeking challenging climbs and technical descents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ancares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How challenging are the mountain bike trails in Ancares?
A: Ancares is known for its rugged mountainous terrain, offering a mix of challenging routes. The trails often feature steep gradients, loose surfaces, and rocky paths, catering primarily to experienced riders. For example, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210426493/puerta-de-ancares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Ancares Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210510282/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; are classified as difficult, involving significant elevation gains.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Ancares?
A: The diverse altitude in Ancares means conditions can vary. Generally, spring through autumn offers the most favorable weather for mountain biking, avoiding the colder, potentially snowy conditions at higher elevations in winter. The region's peaks, such as Cuiña and Mustallar, can be quite exposed.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Ancares?
A: While Ancares is largely characterized by challenging terrain, some routes may offer less strenuous sections. However, many trails involve significant climbing and technical descents. It's advisable to check individual route details for difficulty and elevation to ensure suitability for families or less experienced riders. The region's focus is more on demanding routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Ancares?
A: Ancares is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and a haven for wildlife, including endangered species like the Cantabrian brown bear and capercaillie. While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially in areas with livestock or sensitive ecosystems. Always check local signage.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see while mountain biking in Ancares?
A: Ancares offers stunning natural beauty. You can expect to see prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mostallar&lt;/a&gt;, deep river valleys, and extensive forests of oak, beech, and chestnut. Many routes provide expansive views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancares Mountains&lt;/a&gt;, particularly from high points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5050169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Ancares&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Ancares?
A: Yes, Ancares features several circular mountain bike routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210426493/puerta-de-ancares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Ancares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a lengthy ride, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210510282/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211455428/as-nogais-desde-piedrafita-circular-por-la-zona-especial-de-conservacion-los-ancares-courel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a special conservation area.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in Ancares?
A: Mountain bike rides in Ancares can vary significantly in duration depending on the route's length and difficulty. Shorter, challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210510282/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; can take around 3-4 hours, while longer, more arduous journeys such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210426493/puerta-de-ancares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Ancares Loop&lt;/a&gt; can exceed 6 hours due to their significant distance and elevation gain.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Ancares?
A: Ancares has historically been isolated, preserving traditional ways of life. While not a densely populated area, you can find rural accommodation and local eateries in villages throughout the region. These often offer traditional cuisine and a chance to experience the local culture. It's advisable to plan ahead, especially during off-peak seasons, as options may be limited in some areas.

Q: Do I need any special permits or have to follow specific regulations for mountain biking in Ancares?
A: As a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, Ancares has regulations in place to protect its natural environment and wildlife. While general access for mountain biking is permitted on designated trails, it's always wise to be aware of any specific local rules, especially concerning protected areas or private land. Respecting the natural environment and staying on marked paths is crucial.

Q: What makes Ancares a unique destination for mountain biking?
A: Ancares stands out for its unique blend of challenging, rugged terrain and pristine natural beauty within a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region offers a chance to ride through untouched landscapes, past traditional 'pallozas' (circular huts), and along routes that have even featured in professional races like the Vuelta a España. Its rich biodiversity and cultural heritage add to the distinct experience.

Q: Are there options for less experienced mountain bikers in Ancares?
A: Ancares is predominantly known for its demanding mountain biking, with many routes featuring significant elevation and technical sections. While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the provided data, some 'moderate' options might exist, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38146303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Faba – The English Hospital loop from Las Herrerías de Valcarce&lt;/a&gt;. It's important for less experienced riders to carefully review route profiles, including distance, elevation, and terrain descriptions, to choose trails that match their skill level.

Q: How well-maintained are the roads and trails for mountain biking in Ancares?
A: The roads in Ancares, particularly those leading to high points like Puerto de Ancares, are generally in good condition with very little traffic, making for an enjoyable and safe riding experience. However, some mountain bike trails, especially in more remote sections or those traversing natural terrain, may include unpaved portions, loose surfaces, and rocky paths, reflecting the region's rugged character.
tips: Piornedo is a magical mountain town with unique traditional houses. Well worth the visit to see the heritage and history of mountain life.
One of the most famous mountain passes in Galicia, bordering the Cantabrian mountain range at about 1,500 meters above sea level, the views on a clear day are spectacular.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for the La Puerta de Ancares mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging mountain bike ride, classified as 'difficult'. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for intense sections, including serious double-digit ramps and sustained gradients exceeding 8%, where pushing your bike may be necessary. The technical difficulty ranges between STS S3 and S6 on the Single Track Scale.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the La Puerta de Ancares circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 63.7 km long with over 2,200 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 6.5 to 7 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved sections (about 34 km), asphalt (around 16 km), and flat stones (about 13.7 km). While the road from Piornedo generally starts wide and in good condition, it becomes narrower and rougher as you climb higher. Some ascents may include gravel sections that can present traction challenges on steep gradients.

faqs.question: What kind of views and scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The ride offers spectacular scenery. You'll start in Piornedo, known for its unique traditional thatched-roof houses, called pallozas. As you ascend, you'll be treated to breathtaking views from the Ancares Pass. The route traverses the Ancares National Reserve, revealing beautiful forested areas, dramatic rock formations, and panoramic vistas over the Ancares valley, Galicia, and Asturias. At higher elevations, you might spot cirques and glacial lakes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Piornedo?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Piornedo. You should be able to find parking within the village, though it's always a good idea to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the La Puerta de Ancares mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Sierra de Los Ancares. However, as this route is within a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and respect other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the higher elevations are less likely to be affected by snow or ice. Always check local weather forecasts before you go, as conditions can change rapidly in the mountains.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or is there an entrance fee for mountain biking in the Sierra de Los Ancares Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: As the route is located within the Reserva da Biosfera Os Ancares Lucenses e Montes de Cervantes, Navia e Becerreá, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails. However, it's important to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and adhere to any local regulations regarding protected areas. Always check for current information from local authorities before your trip.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths or routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of the [CIMA LU12] Ancares * Piornedo, [CIMA LU10] Campa das Laviadas - Murias do Camín, [CIMA LU12] Ancares * Pan do Zarco, and the Transcantábrica routes, as well as [CIMA LU12] Ancares * Valouta.

faqs.question: Are there any notable historical or cultural sites along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route starts in Piornedo Traditional Thatched-Roof Houses, a magical mountain town famous for its unique pallozas. These ancient Celtic-origin dwellings are a significant part of the region's cultural heritage and offer a fascinating glimpse into traditional life in the Ancares mountains.
